module_mood_contract: joyful creation — 'it works, on my repo'

rules_evaluated.evidence: getting-going.md line 35 confirms placement after all three exercises (orient-and-introspect, fix-tests-first, compound-and-close). Maintainer block: 'closes M1, after compound-and-close (Ex3) and before the Bridge to M2.' Lecture names the pattern (compound engineering) the student just lived — canonical closer shape.

This lecture is a closer, not a pre-exercise opener. M1 is not a contrast-mood module (contrast-mood is M5's shape). Rule 2 fires on dosage of lecturing before contrast-mood exercises; this lecture sits after all exercises as the M1 session cap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Pure meta-frame lecture — no fenced prompts, no exercises, no forcing functions present. Rule fires on exercise phases with fenced forcing functions; not applicable to a lecture body with no prompt blocks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches work; trainer manages room. No authored conversations or pause cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Scanned full body: zero 'discuss with,' 'share with,' 'pair with,' 'take 90 seconds,' 'tell the room' constructions. Body is pure first-person storytelling about the training's construction. No authored cohort-management beats.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood lands per phase (8+/10 on teaching beats).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Whole body is a single TEACHING beat (not setup). Sim trace phase_score: 9/10. 'That shape you just heard... is the shape you just ran on your own repo' — mirroring works cleanly for a persona who just ran the loop 90 min ago. Mood stays in joyful creation register; does not resolve into M2's grounded competence.

Lecture frames wrongness as productive: 'wrong in the way any first version is wrong' (line 7), 'wrong in specific, namable ways' (line 14), 'That gap turned out to be the valuable part' (line 9). No shame register anywhere. Unease = epistemic not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Section headings name positive disciplines: 'Wrong is how steering gets in,' 'The rules started doing the work,' 'Then the agents started checking the agents,' 'What you just did.' Big Idea at close is compound engineering (Klaassen) — a named positive discipline. No failure-mode title, no failure-mode lead.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity: M1 Big Idea honored.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M1 Big Idea (getting-going.md): 'The first compound loop closes on a trivial bug from your own backlog.' Lecture closes that loop by naming the pattern and citing Klaassen. The lecture IS the Big Idea's summation — names what the student just did and hands it back as a recognisable loop. No competing frame smuggled in.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: M1 close hands off to M2 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture close: 'The next module will extend it. The module after that will extend it again.' M1 module file Next section bridges to M2 plan-mode. Lecture does not pre-empt M2's grounded-competence teaching moment — leaves the rules file in place as the handoff artifact without explaining what M2 will do with it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early: joyful creation stays in register, does not preempt M2's grounded competence.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Close score 9/10 — joyful creation peaks, does not flatten into M2's 'I can feel when a plan is good' competence. 'Mental models only come from doing. You just did.' registers as recognition joy, not mastery-arrival. No plan-mode mention, no multi-file preview — M2's teaching moment is intact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across persona variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key teaching moment: 'That shape you just heard... is the shape you just ran on your own repo.' Depends on student having completed Ex3 (compound-and-close) before the lecture lands. For the target persona (just ran the loop), recognition fires reliably. Delivery risk if timing breaks (trainer runs lecture before Ex3) is a trainer-pacing issue, not a body failure — the arc position in getting-going.md places it correctly after Ex3.
